question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the next pagination link returned by getPaginationLink() by modifying the spender.id and token.id cursor values in subsequent requests, causing the server to return allowance records outside the originally intended range and revealing additional spender relationships for a staking account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance endpoint to enumerate all token IDs for which they are a spender on a victim's account, and then use the token with the highest liquidity to execute a transferFrom that triggers the victim's staking reward payout, with the attacker as the recipient of both the tokens and the indirectly triggered reward?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with both spender.id=gte:X and spender.id=lte:Y to define a spender range, causing the multi-union query to execute three subqueries (lower, inner, upper) that together return more rows than the limit parameter would suggest, enabling bulk data extraction about staking account allowances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query the token allowances of a contract account that is staking to a node, discover which EOAs have been granted allowances over the contract's tokens, and use one of those allowances to trigger a transaction on the contract that forces a staking reward payout from the contract's pending_reward balance?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validateSecondaryBound()] Can an unprivileged external user supply only a token.id filter without a spender.id filter, expecting validateSecondaryBound() to throw an error, but instead exploit any edge case in the validation logic where the error message reveals information about the account's token holdings that can be used to identify high-value staking tokens?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance API to confirm that a token allowance they obtained through a legitimate transaction is still valid, and then use that allowance to execute a series of small transferFrom transactions that cumulatively trigger multiple staking reward payouts, each time resetting the victim's stake_period_start and preventing future reward accumulation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with order=asc and spender.id=gte:0 to retrieve all token allowances in ascending spender order, and use the amount_granted field to identify which spenders have the largest allowances, targeting the highest-allowance spender to execute a maximum-value transferFrom that triggers the largest possible staking reward payout?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a 0x-prefixed EVM address as the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress parameter to trigger the EVM address resolution path in EntityService.getEncodedId(), and if the address resolves to a staking account, use the returned allowances to identify exploitable spender relationships for staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the inner subquery in the multi-union uses a strict gt/lt operator (not gte/lte) for the primary bound, causing a gap in the result set that omits allowances at the exact boundary values, and use this gap to infer the existence of allowances that are not returned, helping the attacker identify hidden spender relationships?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance API to monitor the amount field of their own allowance across multiple requests, detecting when the victim has revoked or reduced the allowance, and racing to execute a transferFrom before the revocation is processed, triggering a staking reward payout that the attacker captures?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query the token allowances of a victim account using the account's alias (base32-encoded public key), and if the alias resolves to a staking account, use the returned spender list to identify which accounts have been granted allowances and can be used to trigger staking reward payouts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with limit=100 (maximum) and no filters to retrieve all token allowances for a victim account in a single request, and use the complete allowance list to identify the spender with the most tokens approved, then use that spender to execute a large transferFrom that triggers a staking reward payout?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the token allowance API does not require authentication, allowing them to query any account's allowances without restriction, and use this to systematically scan all accounts for large pending_reward values combined with active token allowances, building a target list for staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validateBoundsRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both a range filter (spender.id=gte:X) and an equal filter (spender.id=X) for the same parameter, expecting validateBoundsRange() to throw an error, but exploit any timing or ordering issue in the validation that causes the query to execute with both filters, returning a narrower result set that confirms the existence of a specific spender relationship?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance API to identify all token IDs for which a staking account has granted allowances, and then use the token with the highest market value to execute a transferFrom that triggers a staking reward payout, maximizing the value of the stolen reward relative to the cost of the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with spender.id=gte:X and token.id=gte:Y where X and Y are the exact values of the last returned row from a previous request, causing the multi-union query to return duplicate rows at the boundary, leaking information about the allowance structure of a staking account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance API to confirm that a token allowance they hold on a victim's staking account has not been revoked, and then use that allowance to execute a transferFrom at the end of a staking period to force a reward payout, with the attacker timing the transaction to occur when the pending_reward is at its maximum value?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a shard.realm.evmAddress format string as the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress parameter to EntityService.getEncodedId(), causing it to resolve to an account that the attacker does not own, and use the returned token allowances to identify spenders who can be used to trigger staking reward payouts on that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the lower, inner, and upper subqueries are combined with UNION ALL (not UNION), potentially returning duplicate allowance records at boundary values, and use these duplicates to infer the exact spender and token_id values at the boundaries of the allowance set for a staking account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance API to enumerate all spenders for a victim account, and then for each spender, check if that spender is also a staking account with a large pending_reward, identifying a chain of staking relationships that can be exploited to trigger cascading reward payouts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance API to confirm the amount_granted value for a specific token allowance they hold, and then execute a transferFrom for exactly that amount to fully drain the allowance, triggering a staking reward payout on the victim's account while simultaneously depleting the victim's token balance?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with spender.id=lte:0 to retrieve allowances for spenders with IDs at or below 0, potentially matching system accounts or special accounts that have been granted allowances over staking-related tokens, and use this information to understand the reward distribution mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance API to identify all token IDs for which they are a spender on multiple victim accounts simultaneously, and then execute coordinated transferFrom transactions on all victim accounts at the same time to trigger simultaneous staking reward payouts, maximizing the total stolen reward value?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validateLowerBounds()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with token.id=gte:Y but spender.id=gt:X (not gte), causing validateLowerBounds() to throw an error that reveals the exact error message format, and use this information to craft subsequent requests that bypass the validation and return allowance data outside the intended range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token allowance API to monitor the timestamp field of a token allowance over time, detecting when the allowance was last modified, and use this timing information to infer when the victim last interacted with their staking account, helping the attacker time their own transactions to maximize stolen pending_reward?
